---
domain: adapters
related: [grid-core, grid-features]
---

# Framework Adapters — Mental Model

## shared-architecture (all three follow same pattern)

- PATTERN: global singleton adapter registered at module load (React/Vue) or DI setup (Angular)
- FLOW: adapter registered → grid parses light DOM → user sets columns with framework components → processConfig() wraps components in bridge functions → grid calls renderer → bridge mounts framework component into cell container
- INVARIANT: adapter must be registered before grid parses light DOM
- INVARIANT: one adapter per app (singleton)

## adapter-interface (all implement)

```
processConfig(config) → config   // wrap framework components in render bridge functions
createComponentRenderer(component) → (ctx) => FrameworkNode
createComponentEditor(component) → (ctx) => FrameworkNode
releaseCell(element) → void      // cleanup when cell removed from DOM
```

## react-adapter

- OWNS: columnRegistries WeakMap, fieldRegistries Map (fallback), portal manager
- BRIDGE: React portal — `createPortal(component, container)` mounts React component into grid cell DOM
- FLOW: `<DataGrid columns={[{field:'status', renderer: StatusBadge}]}>` → processGridConfig wraps StatusBadge → grid calls renderer → PortalManager.render() mounts into cell container
- TENSION: WeakMap lookups fail on element recreation (framework re-renders create new DOM); field-name fallback lookup adds complexity
- KEY FILES: react-grid-adapter.ts, portal-bridge.ts, react-column-config.ts, use-grid.ts
- DECIDED (#256, Apr 2026): `createNodeBridge<TCtx>(reactFn)` exported from `portal-bridge.ts` is the canonical wrapper for any `(ctx) => ReactNode` → `HTMLElement | null` bridge. Builds a `display:contents` wrapper, calls `renderToContainer`, and passes through `null`/`undefined`/`false` returns as `null`. Used by `features/pinned-rows.ts`, `features/grouping-columns.ts`, `features/grouping-rows.ts`. Renderers that core requires to always return an element coerce via `bridged(ctx) ?? document.createElement('div')`. `features/filtering.ts` is intentionally INLINE — its per-container key tracking + `appendChild` shape (vs. setting innerHTML) doesn't fit the shared helper without forking it.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, regression fix): The `<tbw-grid>` inline `ref` callback in `data-grid.tsx` performs the initial sync of `gridConfig`/`rows`/`columns` ONCE, gated by a `initialSyncDoneRef = useRef(false)`. WHY: React detaches and reattaches inline ref callbacks on every render (calling the ref with `null`, then with the element again). Without the gate, every parent re-render — for example a `useDynamicHeight` hook firing a `ResizeObserver` and producing a new `style` object — re-assigned `grid.gridConfig = processedGridConfig`. Even though the reference was the same memoized object, ConfigManager's `setGridConfig` flipped `sourcesChanged = true` (now defended by an identity short-circuit there as well, see `grid-core.md` config-manager), so the next `merge()` rebuilt `effectiveConfig.columns` from the original `gridConfig.columns` — wiping runtime mutations like `col.hidden` written by `setColumnVisible()` / `applyColumnState()`. Symptom: column visibility / column state silently reset whenever a parent re-rendered for any unrelated reason. A naive `if (!previous)` gate is INSUFFICIENT — React's detach pass leaves `previous === null`, so the next attach still triggers the body. The `useRef(false)` flag survives detach/reattach. Tests: `data-grid.spec.tsx > does not re-assign gridConfig on parent re-render (regression)`.

## vue-adapter

- OWNS: columnRegistries WeakMap, fieldRegistries Map (fallback), teleport manager
- BRIDGE: Vue teleport — mounts VNode into grid cell container
- FLOW: `<TbwGrid :columns="[{field:'status', renderer: StatusBadge}]">` → processGridConfig wraps → teleport bridge → VNode rendered
- COMPONENT DETECTION: checks `__name` (SFC), `setup` function (Composition), `render` function (Options), or plain function (Functional)
- KEY FILES: vue-grid-adapter.ts, teleport-manager.ts, vue-column-config.ts, use-grid.ts, TbwGrid.vue
- INVARIANT: TbwGrid.vue MUST mount a `<TeleportManager>` and register it via `setTeleportManager(gridEl, handle)` before any teleport-bridge call. Without registration, `renderToContainer()` falls back to `createApp()` and silently severs `provide`/`inject`, Pinia, Router, i18n contexts. The fallback path is reachable when adapters render directly (e.g. tool panels, master-detail) — not just user-supplied cell renderers.
- INVARIANT: TbwGrid.vue MUST call `nextTick(() => { masterDetail.refreshDetailRenderer?.(); responsive.refreshCardRenderer?.(); refreshColumns(); refreshShellHeader(); })` in `onMounted`. Light-DOM children declared in the parent template (e.g. `<TbwGridToolPanel>`, `<TbwGridResponsiveCard>`) mount AFTER the grid's first scan; without these calls the framework children are silently ignored.
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, #237): `createToolPanelRenderer` uses a "wrapper-detach" pattern instead of a `sync` flag on teleport-bridge. The renderer creates an inner `<div class="vue-tool-panel">`, teleports VNodes into it, and on cleanup synchronously calls `wrapper.remove()` (detaches from container) BEFORE scheduling the async teleport-removal microtask. Rationale: Vue 3.5 has no `flushSync` equivalent (unlike React's `react-dom/flushSync`), so we cannot synchronously force teleport unmount. Detaching the wrapper makes the shell's downstream `container.innerHTML = ''` (accordion-collapse path in `shell.ts:830` and `shell-controller.ts:187`) a no-op, while Vue's deferred unmount still finds its children attached to the (now-orphaned) wrapper and unmounts cleanly without `NotFoundError`. The wrapper is GC'd after Vue completes. Localizes the fix without polluting `teleport-bridge.ts` / `teleport-manager.ts` with an opt-in flag that would have to be plumbed through every callsite.
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, #237 follow-up): Vue reuses core `createPluginsFromFeatures` from `@toolbox-web/grid/features/registry` — same source of truth as React's `use-sync-plugins`. Gets plugin dependency validation + dep-ordered instantiation for free. Do NOT reintroduce hand-rolled feature-plugin loops in the Vue adapter; new features get picked up automatically once registered.
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, #237 follow-up): `columns` prop accepts `ColumnShorthand<TRow>[]` (string | number | `ColumnConfig`) and a sibling `columnDefaults: Partial<ColumnConfig<TRow>>` prop merges before individual column props. Shorthand helpers live in `libs/grid-vue/src/lib/column-shorthand.ts` and mirror React's; the extra `applyColumnDefaults` helper handles the per-column merge.
- DECIDED (#256, Apr 2026): `createNodeBridge<TCtx>(vueFn)` exported from `teleport-bridge.ts` is the canonical wrapper for any `(ctx) => VNode | null | undefined` → `HTMLElement | null` bridge. Mirrors React's helper exactly (display:contents wrapper + `renderToContainer` + null passthrough). Used by `features/pinned-rows.ts`, `features/grouping-columns.ts`, `features/grouping-rows.ts`. Same `?? document.createElement('div')` coercion for non-nullable renderers; `features/filtering.ts` left inline for the same reason as React.

## adapter-conformance (cross-adapter)

- OWNS: `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS` (grid core, `libs/grid/src/lib/core/adapter-conformance.ts`) — canonical list of 10 `FrameworkAdapter` methods that the grid core invokes. Re-exported from `public.ts`.
- INVARIANT: every adapter package has `<framework>-grid-adapter.conformance.spec.ts` that iterates `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS` and fails when any entry resolves to a non-function on the adapter instance (Vue/React) or prototype (Angular — ctor needs DI).
- INVARIANT: when adding a new hook the grid core depends on, append to `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS` so every adapter's conformance spec goes red until it's implemented (or explicitly documented as intentionally omitted).
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, #237 follow-up): only the `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS` constant is exported from `public.ts`. The helper functions `assertAdapterConformance`/`reportAdapterConformance` stay internal — exporting them pushed `index.js` over the (then 45 kB) gzipped budget. Even with the budget raised to 50 kB hard / 45 kB warn (Apr 2026), the same logic applies: each adapter spec inlines the 2-line filter; DRY is not worth the bundle cost.
- TENSION (Angular): spec MUST start with `import '@angular/compiler';` because the adapter transitively imports `@angular/forms` (partially-compiled, needs JIT at test time). Angular spec uses `GridAdapter.prototype` instead of `new GridAdapter()` because the constructor requires `EnvironmentInjector` / `ApplicationRef` / `ViewContainerRef`.
- FOUND (Nov 2025): this conformance test surfaced that React AND Angular were both missing `parseResponsiveCardElement` — fixed by adding a 1-line delegation to `createResponsiveCardRenderer` via `closest('tbw-grid')`.

## angular-adapter

- OWNS: viewRefs (embedded views), componentRefs, per-cell editor tracking
- BRIDGE: Angular embedded views — `createEmbeddedView(template, ctx)` + `syncRootNodes(viewRef, container)`
- FLOW: `<tbw-grid-column field="status"><app-badge *tbwRenderer="let value" /></tbw-grid-column>` → directive registers template → adapter creates embedded view → syncRootNodes appends to cell
- SYNC ROOT NODES: compares viewRef.rootNodes vs container.childNodes; replaces all if different (handles @if/@for control flow blocks)
- TENSION: two syntaxes (\*tbwRenderer structural directive vs tbw-grid-column-view nested directive) both supported
- TENSION: registration requires manual setup in AppComponent (not automatic like React/Vue)
- KEY FILES: angular-grid-adapter.ts, angular-column-config.ts, inject-grid.ts
- DECIDED (#256, Apr 2026): `mountComponentRenderer<TCtx>(componentClass, mapInputs, pool='render'|'editor')` (in `angular-grid-adapter.ts`) is the canonical primitive for mounting a standalone Angular component into a grid cell/header/panel. Returns `(ctx) => { hostElement, componentRef }` so callers needing the `ComponentRef` (editor wiring, change detection) still get it. All `createComponent*Renderer` methods (`createComponentRenderer`, `createComponentEditor`, `createComponentHeaderRenderer`, `createComponentFilterPanelRenderer`, etc.) are now thin wrappers around this primitive — composition not generalization. Composed AROUND it: WeakMap `cellCache` (renderer pool reuse) and editor wiring (`wireEditorCallbacks`, `attachBeforeEditCloseFlush`, `onValueChange`). Do NOT try to fold these into the primitive — they're concerns of specific call sites, not bridging.
- DECIDED (May 2026, refactor branch `refactor/grid-angular-feature-directives`, pilot): The Angular adapter migrates each plugin's `[input]` + `(output)` off the central `Grid` directive into an attribute-selector directive in the matching `features/<name>/src/grid-<name>.directive.ts`. WHY: each `input()` / `output()` on `Grid` ships compiled `ɵɵdefineDirective` metadata + signal-factory code into the core bundle whether the consumer imported the feature or not — 22 plugins × ~2 bindings each made grid-angular core 239 kB / 50.5 kB gz vs. grid-react 16.5 kB gz. The new pattern (e.g. `@Directive({ selector: 'tbw-grid[filtering], tbw-grid[filterChange]' })`) lets the typed surface tree-shake with the rest of the feature when it isn't in the consumer's `imports`. Compile-time guarantee replaces runtime guard — Angular's compiler errors with `Can't bind to 'filtering' since it isn't a known property of 'tbw-grid'` if the directive isn't imported. STRONGER than React/Vue (which silently drop unknown props) and stronger than the WC core (runtime throw). RULED OUT: Angular host directives — `hostDirectives` is static metadata baked into `Grid.ɵɵdefineDirective`, no runtime API to mutate, listing all 22 there breaks tree-shaking. ALSO RULED OUT: side-effect auto-registration of directives — Angular's standalone template compiler resolves directives statically from the consuming component's `imports` array, never consults DI/runtime/registries (this is intentional, deprecated with `BrowserModule`).
- DECIDED (May 2026, hybrid co-existence rule): To avoid a v2 breaking change today, deprecated `Grid` inputs/outputs stay alongside new feature directives. Co-existence is mediated by a framework-free per-element claims registry (`libs/grid-angular/src/lib/internal/feature-claims.ts`, `WeakMap<HTMLElement, Map<name, configGetter>>` + `WeakMap<HTMLElement, Set<eventName>>`). Feature directive constructor calls `registerFeatureClaim(grid, name, () => this.input())` + `claimEvent(grid, eventName)`; `Grid.createFeaturePlugins` reads `getFeatureClaim(grid, name)?.()` first (falling back to its deprecated input), and `Grid.setupEventListeners` skips `isEventClaimed` events. Reading the claim's getter inside the effect transitively reads the directive's signal so reactivity propagates. Constructor-ordering safety: all directives at a node are constructed before any `effect()` fires (effects run in the next microtask), so claims are in place before `Grid` first computes plugins. Cleanup: directive's `ngOnDestroy` calls `unregisterFeatureClaim` + `unclaimEvent` so removing the directive via `*ngIf` lets the deprecated `Grid` binding take back over without leaving stale ownership. Foundation cost (claims module + barrel exports + consultation logic in `Grid`): +5.5 kB raw / +1.5 kB gz on grid-angular core, paid once. Subsequent feature directives only grow their own feature bundles. v2 will delete every deprecated input/output + the registry consultation + the `eventOutputMap` plugin entries from `Grid`, dropping core toward grid-react/vue territory.
- DECIDED (May 2026, claim-vs-input precedence): When both a feature directive and a deprecated `Grid` input are present (user binds `[filtering]` AND has `GridFilteringDirective` in `imports`), the directive wins. Both Angular bindings still fire — the compiler matches the binding against every directive whose selector applies and assigns to each — but `Grid.createFeaturePlugins` reads from the claim's signal, not its own input, so only one plugin is created. For events the directive owns the listener; `Grid` skips its `eventOutputMap` entry for that event so its deprecated `output()` never emits. WHY directive wins (not last-write-wins): the directive is the supported v2 API. Inverting the precedence would mean removing the directive's claim breaks back-compat in the opposite direction. KNOWN ASYMMETRY: deprecated `Grid` outputs subscribed via `(filterChange)` will go silent for that grid the moment a `GridFilteringDirective` is imported into the same component — by design (single source of truth) and called out in the directive's JSDoc.
- TENSION (May 2026, registry barrel export cost): The claims registry helpers (`registerFeatureClaim`, `claimEvent`, etc.) are re-exported from the `@toolbox-web/grid-angular` package barrel because ng-packagr forbids relative imports from secondary entries (`features/<name>/src/...`) into the primary entry's `rootDir`. Exposing them as public API prevents tree-shaking the helpers themselves out of the core bundle even for consumers that import zero feature directives. Marked `@internal` in JSDoc and excluded from typedoc (TODO: verify the typedoc exclusion). The alternative — duplicating the registry per feature entry — loses cross-grid claim isolation. The barrel approach is the lesser evil.

## event-handling

| Framework | Pattern                                  | Mechanism                                          |
| --------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| React     | props: `onCellClick={(d) => ...}`        | event-props.ts maps prop → native addEventListener |
| Vue       | emits: `@cell-click="handler"`           | props → native addEventListener                    |
| Angular   | outputs: `(cellClick)="handler($event)"` | directive wires native events to Angular outputs   |

- DECIDED: editor `before-edit-close` → native `.blur()` on focused input. React adapter (`react-grid-adapter.ts`) and Vue adapter (`vue-grid-adapter.ts`) both attach a `before-edit-close` listener on the host `<tbw-grid>` from `createEditor`, and on fire call `focused.blur()` if the active element is an input/textarea/select inside the editor container. WHY: Tab and programmatic row exits rebuild the cell DOM synchronously, so editors written with `onBlur={commit}` / `@blur="commit"` would otherwise lose pending input. Native `.blur()` is required (not `dispatchEvent(new FocusEvent('blur'))`) because React 17+ delegates focus events at the root by listening to the bubbling `focusout`, not the non-bubbling `blur` — `.blur()` dispatches both. Angular's `BaseGridEditor.onBeforeEditClose()` hook is the parallel pattern (subclasses override to call `commitValue()`). Cleanup: per-container teardown in `releaseCell` + bulk teardown in `dispose()`/`cleanup()`.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, follow-up to PR #249): The `before-edit-close` → `.blur()` bridge MUST also be wired into the **config-path** editor wrappers, not only the slot/JSX `createEditor` path. Specifically: React `wrapReactEditor` (in `react-column-config.ts`), Vue `createConfigVNodeEditor` + `createConfigComponentEditor` + `createTypeEditor` (via shared `attachBeforeEditCloseFlush` helper), and Angular `createComponentEditor` + the template path `createEditor` (via shared `attachBeforeEditCloseFlush` helper). WHY: Tab from the last editable cell of a row triggers `EditingPlugin.preventDefault()` + `#exitRowEdit(currentRow, false)` → `beginBulkEdit(nextRow)` synchronously — the focused input never blurs naturally, so editors written with `onBlur={commit}` / `@blur="commit"` / `(blur)="commit()"` silently discard pending input. The grid element is resolved lazily via `queueMicrotask` because the editor container is appended to the cell _after_ the wrapper returns. Cleanup: React threads an `unsub` field through `mountedPortals` entries (called in `cleanupConfigRootsIn`); Vue/Angular store unsubs in an `editorBeforeCloseUnsubs` Map keyed by container/host, swept in `releaseCell` (cell-scoped) and `destroy()` (bulk). Tests: `react-column-config.spec.ts > flushes the focused input on before-edit-close`, `vue-grid-adapter.spec.ts > flushes the focused input on before-edit-close for config-based VNode editor`, `angular-grid-adapter.spec.ts > before-edit-close blur bridge` (template + component + releaseCell).
- DECIDED (issue #250 — React renderer+editor `removeChild` crash): The grid core MUST call `grid.__frameworkAdapter?.releaseCell?.(cell)` BEFORE any synchronous wipe (`innerHTML = ''` / `replaceChildren()`) of a cell that may contain adapter-managed renderer DOM. `editor-injection.ts` does this when opening an editor. WHY: a `cell.innerHTML = ''` removes React-mounted `.react-cell-renderer` nodes without notifying React; the fiber tree still references the orphan DOM, and the next React commit (e.g. user `setRows` from `onCellCommit`) throws `NotFoundError: Failed to execute 'removeChild' on 'Node'`. The React adapter's `releaseCell` resolves the cell's portal containers via `cellEl.querySelectorAll('.react-cell-renderer, .react-cell-editor')` + a `containerToKey` reverse index (O(cell descendants), not O(total portals)), calling `removeFromContainer(key, { sync: true })` + `untrackPortal(key)`, then `cleanupConfigRootsIn(cellEl)` (which uses the same DOM-scoped lookup against a `Map<HTMLElement, MountedEntry>` keyed by container). DEFENSE IN DEPTH: both `wrapReactRenderer` (in `react-column-config.ts`) and `createRenderer` (in `react-grid-adapter.ts`) validate `cellEl.contains(cached.container)` before reusing a `cellCache` WeakMap entry; if detached, they sync-tear-down the stale React root and create a fresh container. Tests: `react-column-config.spec.ts > evicts cache and creates fresh container...`, `react-grid-adapter.spec.ts > evicts stale cache...` and `> also unmounts renderer portals inside the cell`.
- DECIDED (issue #250 follow-up — row-recycle path): The `releaseCell` invariant ALSO covers the two grid-core paths that detach cell DOM **without** opening an editor: (1) the pool-shrink branch in `renderVisibleRows` (`libs/grid/src/lib/core/internal/rows.ts`) calls `releaseCell` for each cell of any pool element that's about to be `el.remove()`-d; (2) `_clearRowPool` (`libs/grid/src/lib/core/grid.ts`) walks every row's children and calls `releaseCell` before `_bodyEl.innerHTML = ''`. Both are guarded by `cell.firstElementChild` so default-rendered cells skip the call. WHY: when a consumer's `onCellCommit` calls `setRows` with a row whose `getRowId` returns a different value (e.g. swapping a temporary negative id for a server-assigned positive one), `renderVisibleRows`'s recycle path may shrink the pool, and config-driven viewport changes (column add/remove, light-DOM re-parse) call `_clearRowPool` — both detach renderer-portal containers without going through `editor-injection.ts`. Without the per-cell `releaseCell`, the React adapter's `portalsRef` still tracks those containers; the next-frame `schedulePrune` rAF in `portal-manager.tsx` finds them disconnected and `flushSync(forceRender)` throws `removeChild`. Tests: `rows.spec.ts > releases cells when pool shrinks`.
- DECIDED (issue #250 second follow-up — per-portal error boundary, Apr 2026): `PortalManager` (`libs/grid-react/src/lib/portal-manager.tsx`) wraps each `createPortal` subtree in a class-component error boundary (`PortalBoundary`) whose `componentDidCatch` drops the offending entry from `portalsRef` and re-renders. WHY: even with all known `releaseCell` paths covered, third-party editors (EDS Autocomplete, popover-API widgets) can move their DOM out from under React mid-edit. The next React commit then throws `NotFoundError: Failed to execute 'removeChild' on 'Node'` from `commitDeletionEffectsOnFiber`. **In React 18+/19, exceptions thrown during a `flushSync` commit are routed to the nearest _descendant_ error boundary's `componentDidCatch`, NOT re-thrown synchronously to the `flushSync` caller** (see React's `defaultOnCaughtError`). A `try/catch` around `flushSync(forceRender)` therefore CANNOT recover — by the time `flushSync` returns, the boundary state has been mutated and the host app's outermost boundary (e.g. React Router's `RenderErrorBoundary`) has already taken over. The per-portal boundary is the right contract: it sits between the failing subtree and the host app, absorbing the throw, dropping the dead key, and letting other portals + the host app keep working. Replaced the original (ineffective) `try/catch` in `schedulePrune` with a plain `forceRender()`; added a render-time filter that skips portals whose `container.isConnected === false`. Tests: `portal-manager.spec.ts > should isolate a crashing portal so other portals keep working (#250)`. KNOWN WEAKNESSES (not currently triggering crashes thanks to the boundary, but flagged for future hardening): `GroupingRowsPlugin.ts` and `pinned-rows.ts` have `rowEl.innerHTML = ''` / `container.innerHTML = ''` paths that do NOT call `releaseCell` first — if a grouped or pinned row ever holds a React-rendered cell, it will rely on the boundary as the only line of defense.

## feature-prop-bridging (all three)

- All adapters expose feature props that auto-load plugins
- React: `<DataGrid selection="range" editing="dblclick" />`
- Vue: `<TbwGrid :selection="'range'" :editing="'dblclick'" />`
- Angular: `<tbw-grid [selection]="'range'" [editing]="'dblclick'">`
- MECHANISM: feature props passed to createPluginsFromFeatures() → factory creates plugins → added to gridConfig.plugins

## programmatic-access

| Framework | API                          | Returns                                           |
| --------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| React     | `useGrid<TRow>()` hook       | ref, isReady, element, getConfig(), forceLayout() |
| Vue       | `useGrid<TRow>()` composable | same (with Ref wrappers)                          |
| Angular   | `injectGrid()` function      | same interface                                    |

## cross-adapter-tensions

- REGISTRATION TIMING: React/Vue auto-register at module load (synchronous); Angular requires explicit DI setup
- WEAKMAP FALLBACK: dual lookup (WeakMap → field name → factory) needed because framework re-renders can create new DOM elements
- PORTAL/TELEPORT OVERHEAD: mounting framework components into web component cells requires bridge layer that manages lifecycle outside framework's component tree

## angular-overlay-editors (BaseOverlayEditor)

- OWNS: panel element (moved to `document.body` to escape grid overflow clipping), `_abortCtrl: AbortController` for all document-level / grid-level listener cleanup
- DISMISSAL SIGNALS (all call abstract `onOverlayOutsideClick()`, subclass decides commit vs cancel):
  - `pointerdown` on document outside panel + host
  - `tbw-scroll` CustomEvent on the closest `<tbw-grid>` ancestor (dogfoods the public event)
  - Focus observer: cell losing `cell-focus` class via MutationObserver → `hideOverlay()` (different path — cancel, not commit)
- INVARIANT: all listeners share the same `_abortCtrl.signal` so `teardownOverlay()` releases everything in one abort
- INVARIANT: handler must guard on `_isOpen && _panel` — listener is attached eagerly in `initOverlay()` before the panel is shown
- FLOW: `initOverlay(panel)` → AbortController created → pointerdown listener on document → `tbw-scroll` listener on grid host → panel moved to body → registerExternalFocusContainer(panel)
- TENSION: anchor positioning uses CSS Anchor (`anchor-name`) when supported, else `_positionWithJs()` — JS fallback only runs on open, so scroll-while-open without dismissal would float the panel at a stale position. Scroll-dismissal sidesteps needing a live reposition loop.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, #234 follow-up): scroll → dismiss (call `onOverlayOutsideClick()`), not scroll → reposition. Rationale: (a) same semantics as click-outside preserves subclass commit/cancel contracts, (b) avoids coupling overlay to grid scroll cadence, (c) consumes the public `tbw-scroll` API — no shadow-DOM reach-arounds, validates the API dogfood-style.

## react-overlay-editors (useGridOverlay)

- OWNS: nothing (pure hook). DELEGATES to `grid.registerExternalFocusContainer(panel)` / `unregisterExternalFocusContainer(panel)`.
- FLOW: effect runs when `(open && panelRef.current)` truthy → resolves grid via 1) explicit `gridElement` option, 2) `panelRef.current.closest('tbw-grid')`, 3) `GridElementContext` from `<DataGrid>` / `<GridProvider>` → registers panel → cleanup unregisters.
- INVARIANT: portaled panels (e.g. `createPortal(..., document.body)`) cannot use path 2 — `closest()` walks DOM, not React tree. Path 3 (context) is the safety net; React preserves context across portals.
- INVARIANT: hook is intentionally minimal — no synthetic Tab dispatch, no Escape handling, no outside-click. Consumers that need full Angular `BaseOverlayEditor` parity wire those themselves; the grid only needs the focus-container registration to keep the row in edit mode. Bundle cost ~0.1 kB gzipped (issue #251).
- DECIDED (#251, Apr 2026): React adapter does NOT ship a `BaseOverlayEditor`-equivalent class because there is no React class-component idiom. A hook + `ColumnEditorContext.grid` cover the same use cases with less surface. Editors that prefer a class can subclass and call `grid.registerExternalFocusContainer` directly in `componentDidMount`.
- DECIDED (#251, Apr 2026): The ARIA-expanded fallback in `EditingPlugin` (see `grid-plugins.md`) means most React combobox/autocomplete editors work WITHOUT calling `useGridOverlay` at all — provided the trigger sets `aria-expanded="true"` + `aria-controls="<panel-id>"` while open. Use the hook for editors whose overlay does not advertise the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern (color pickers, menus, custom popovers).

## vue-overlay-editors (useGridOverlay)

- OWNS: nothing (pure composable). DELEGATES to `grid.registerExternalFocusContainer(panel)` / `unregisterExternalFocusContainer(panel)`.
- FLOW: `watch({open, panel, grid, ctx}, …, {immediate: true, flush: 'post'})` — on every fire, unregisters previously-registered (panel, grid) pair, then if `(open && panel)` re-resolves grid via 1) explicit `gridElement` option (`MaybeRef<DataGridElement>`), 2) `panel.closest('tbw-grid')`, 3) `inject(GRID_ELEMENT_KEY)` ref populated by `<TbwGrid>` / `<GridProvider>` → registers the new pair. `onScopeDispose` runs the same unregister to cover unmount-while-open.
- INVARIANT: teleported panels (e.g. `<Teleport to="body">`) cannot use path 2 — `closest()` walks DOM, not Vue tree. Path 3 (injection) is the safety net; Vue preserves provide/inject across teleports as long as the teleport is rendered inside a setup() function with the inject in scope.
- INVARIANT: composable accepts `MaybeRef<boolean>` for `open` and `MaybeRef<DataGridElement | null | undefined>` for `gridElement` so callers can pass either reactive refs or plain values. The watch dependency object pattern is required because `unref(open)` inside a getter must be tracked by Vue's reactivity — wrapping in an object lets us include `panel`, `grid`, and (computed) `ctx` as siblings.
- DECIDED (#251 follow-up, Apr 2026): Vue adapter mirrors React's `useGridOverlay` rather than offering a `BaseOverlayEditor`-style class — Vue idioms favour composables, and `ColumnEditorContext.grid` is enough for class-style editors that want to call `grid.registerExternalFocusContainer` directly from `mounted()`. ARIA fallback in `EditingPlugin` covers combobox/autocomplete editors regardless of whether they call the composable.

## vue-teleport-manager (per-entry error boundary)

- OWNS: `teleports: ShallowRef<Map<string, TeleportEntry>>` keyed by stable entry id. Each entry rendered as `<Teleport :to="entry.container" :key="key"><TeleportEntryBoundary :entryKey="key">{{ vnode }}</TeleportEntryBoundary></Teleport>`.
- INVARIANT: `TeleportEntryBoundary` is an inner `defineComponent` with `errorCaptured(err, _instance, info) { …drop entry…; return false; }`. Returning `false` is REQUIRED — it stops the error from propagating to the host app's `app.config.errorHandler` and to ancestor `errorCaptured` hooks. Without it, a single misbehaving cell renderer would surface as a global Vue error.
- INVARIANT: when dropping the entry, the handler MUST replace `teleports.value` with a new `Map` instance (`new Map(teleports.value); copy.delete(key); teleports.value = copy`). `ShallowRef` only fires on identity change; mutating the existing map in place would render the entry indefinitely on the next reactive flush.
- FLOW: cell renderer throws during render → boundary's `errorCaptured` fires → entry dropped from map → console.error logged with entry key + Vue lifecycle info string → next render iteration omits the broken `<Teleport>` → subsequent `removeTeleport(key)` calls on the dropped key are safe no-ops (map.delete on missing key returns false).
- DECIDED (#250/#251 follow-up, Apr 2026): mirrors React's `PortalBoundary` (see `react-overlay-editors` block & line 76 DECIDED entry). Vue's `errorCaptured` is the idiomatic equivalent of React's `componentDidCatch` — both are designed exactly for this "isolate one subtree's failure from siblings and from the host app" use case. Bundle cost ~30 lines / negligible gzipped.

## angular-adapter-testing

- INVARIANT: the angular adapter project deliberately avoids `TestBed`. Bootstrapping the platform-browser-dynamic compiler in unit tests adds 3-5s per spec file and is unnecessary for component-free logic. Spec files document this with a comment near the imports.
- PATTERN: for classes constructed via `inject()` (e.g. `GridTypeRegistry`, `BaseOverlayEditor`), instantiate without running the constructor: `Object.create(MyClass.prototype)` and manually seed the few private fields the constructor would initialise.
- PATTERN: for the `AngularGridAdapter` itself, mock just `createComponent` from `@angular/core` and the seven template-registry getter modules (`./directives/grid-detail-view.directive`, `./directives/grid-responsive-card.directive`, `./directives/grid-tool-panel.directive`, `./directives/grid-column-view.directive`, `./directives/grid-column-editor.directive`, `./directives/structural-directives`, `./grid-type-registry`). The mocked `createComponent` MUST read its `hostElement` option onto `componentRef.location.nativeElement` so the adapter's `cellEl.contains(ref.location.nativeElement)` cleanup checks succeed.
- PATTERN: for directives that use `inject()` + `effect()` + `input()` field initialisers (e.g. `GridFormArray`), `vi.mock('@angular/core', …)` to replace the three primitives with stubs: `inject` dispatches via a per-test resolver, `effect` is a no-op (post-init behaviour is exercised through `grid.on(…)` callbacks captured via `vi.fn().mockImplementation`), `input`/`input.required` returns a getter with a `__setValue` setter so tests can drive signal values. Then `new MyDirective()` works without an Angular injection context.
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, #237 follow-up): when CI coverage is computed by V8, only loaded files appear in the denominator. PR #237's `angular-grid-adapter.conformance.spec.ts` added an `import { GridAdapter }` that pulled the adapter + every directive into the report and dropped the project from ~80% to ~33%. Restoration path is to write real unit tests using the patterns above, NOT to lower the threshold or exclude files. `grid.directive.ts` (the public `<tbw-grid>` directive) is intentionally NOT exercised by unit tests — it is a thin Angular wrapper covered end-to-end by the demo apps and Playwright; importing it would double the project size visible to V8.

## adapter-event-props (drift-safety pattern)

- OWNS (React): hand-maintained `EventProps<TRow>` interface + hand-maintained `EVENT_PROP_MAP` runtime constant in `libs/grid-react/src/lib/event-props.ts`. Both are guarded by `} as const satisfies Readonly<Record<keyof EventProps, keyof DataGridEventMap<unknown>>>;` — bidirectional drift fails compile.
- OWNS (Angular): hand-maintained `output<T>()` signals + hand-maintained `eventOutputMap` runtime constant in `libs/grid-angular/src/lib/directives/grid.directive.ts`. Guarded by `} as const satisfies Readonly<Record<string, keyof DataGridEventMap<unknown>>>;` — value-side only (output names not statically tied to signal fields).
- INVARIANT: NEVER widen the satisfies clause to silence a complaint. The complaint is the feature: if `'foo'` isn't in `keyof DataGridEventMap`, either the event isn't real (drop the prop) OR the event registration is missing (add it to the appropriate `declare module '../../core/types'` augmentation in the plugin's `types.ts`).
- INVARIANT: When the satisfies guard rejects an event name, BEFORE dropping the prop, check the actual emit call sites (`grep_search` for `'event-name'` and `_emit('event-name'`). Plugins emit events via `this._emit(...)` but the `DataGridEventMap` registration is the consumer-facing type contract; the two can diverge silently — the satisfies guard catches that gap, but only the emit-site check tells you which side to fix.
- DECIDED (May 2026): tried fully auto-deriving `EventProps` from `keyof DataGridEventMap` via mapped type. Works at runtime but TypeDoc renders mapped types as `type EventProps = unknown` — losing the per-prop JSDoc table the docs site relies on. Hand-maintained interface + `satisfies` is the right balance: full prop-level docs, full drift safety.
- DECIDED (May 2026, regression fix): `'column-visibility'` was emitted by `config-manager.ts` (`setColumnVisible` / `toggleColumnVisibility` / `showAllColumns`) but NOT registered in `DataGridEventMap`. The satisfies guard rejected `onColumnVisibility` / `columnVisibility` props as "non-existent event" and they were briefly removed. Fix: registered `'column-visibility': ColumnVisibilityDetail` in the visibility plugin's module augmentation (`libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/visibility/types.ts`). Both adapter props came back as fully working API. LESSON: a missing registration looks identical to a non-existent event from the satisfies guard's perspective — always check emit sites before deleting consumer API.
- DECIDED (May 2026): `EventProps` is intentionally a flat interface (one entry per event, even closely-related pairs like `onUndo`/`onRedo`, `onGroupExpand`/`onGroupCollapse`). Tried `onUndoRedo` (single prop fired on either action) — it bound to `'undo-redo'` which the plugin never emits. Plugins emit `'undo'` and `'redo'` separately; the props mirror that. Same for the visibility-sidebar `'column-reorder-request'` (request-only event consumed by ReorderPlugin) vs the actual user-facing `'column-visibility'` event — keep them separate, do not merge into a synthetic "column changed" prop.
- TENSION: Angular's `eventOutputMap` keys cannot today be statically tied to the directive's `output<T>()` signal fields (would require `keyof Grid` filtered to output-typed fields — verbose and brittle). Today, adding a new `output<T>()` requires also adding a map entry; forgetting only causes the listener not to wire (silent runtime bug in dev, no compile error). React's `Record<keyof EventProps, ...>` enforces both directions because `EventProps` is a flat interface.
- TENSION (Angular parity gap, May 2026): React adapter exposes `onColumnResizeReset`, `onDataChange`, `onCellCancel`, `onEditOpen`, `onBeforeEditClose`, `onEditClose`, `onDirtyChange`, `onGroupExpand`, `onGroupCollapse`, `onContextMenuOpen` that Angular does NOT yet expose as outputs. Add as parity follow-up.
- TENSION (Vue): not yet audited for the same drift-safety pattern. Vue users typically use `@event-name` syntax directly (which goes through the typed `addEventListener` overload), so the gap is smaller — but worth a follow-up audit.

## react-vue-parity (May 2026 audit)

- DECIDED (May 2026, parity sweep): React and Vue adapters audited for surface-area parity. Verified identical: 26 feature props, 28 grid events, hooks/composables (`useGrid` / `useGridOverlay`), provider components, tree-shakeable feature registration, portal/teleport bridges, registry types. Adjustments made to bring divergent surfaces in line:
  - **`applyColumnDefaults`**: added to `libs/grid-react/src/lib/column-shorthand.ts` (Vue already had it) and exported from both adapter index files alongside `normalizeColumns` / `parseColumnShorthand` / `hasColumnShorthands`. React's `data-grid.tsx` now calls the helper instead of the inlined merge for consistency.
  - **`cardRowHeight`**: exposed as a prop on `TbwGridResponsiveCard.vue` (React `GridResponsiveCard` already had it). Bound to the underlying `<tbw-grid-responsive-card>` via the kebab-case `card-row-height` attribute that `ResponsivePlugin.parseLightDOM` reads.
  - **`SSRProps`**: re-introduced as a deprecated type in `libs/grid-vue/src/lib/feature-props.ts` and union'd into `AllFeatureProps` so `AllFeatureProps<TRow>` has the same shape on both adapters. The flag itself remains a no-op on both sides — see line 472 React `SSRProps` JSDoc / Vue mirror for the canonical reasoning.
  - **`GridCellContext` / `GridEditorContext`**: exported from `@toolbox-web/grid-vue` as type aliases over `CellSlotProps` / `EditorSlotProps` with React's `<TValue, TRow>` generic order. Inside Vue SFCs `defineSlots` still infers from `*SlotProps`; the new aliases exist for users typing render-prop-style helpers shared across adapters.
  - **`tool-panel-registry.spec.ts`**: created in `libs/grid-vue/src/lib/` to bring registry test coverage in line with `detail-panel-registry.spec.ts` and `responsive-card-registry.spec.ts`.
- INVARIANT: when adding a new public export to one adapter, add the equivalent to the other in the same PR. The two `index.ts` files are the canonical surface; if a consumer can `import { X } from '@toolbox-web/grid-react'`, they should be able to `import { X } from '@toolbox-web/grid-vue'` (modulo idiomatic naming like `DataGrid` ↔ `TbwGrid`).
- INVARIANT: `AllFeatureProps<TRow>` shape MUST be identical across React and Vue. If a feature is added to one side's `FeatureProps`, mirror it to the other; if SSRProps is ever truly removed, remove from both atomically.
- TENSION: idiomatic naming differences are NOT parity gaps — `<DataGrid>` vs `<TbwGrid>`, `children` render props vs `#cell`/`#editor` slots, `GridElementContext` vs `GRID_ELEMENT_KEY`, `PortalManager` vs `TeleportManager` are all framework-natural and stay as-is.

## adapter-feature-props (forward-only drift guard)

- OWNS: `FeatureConfig` (in `@toolbox-web/grid/all`) is augmented by every side-effect feature import (each plugin's `types.ts` augments `FeatureConfig`). It's the canonical core feature registry.
- OWNS (React): hand-maintained `FeatureProps<TRow>` in `libs/grid-react/src/lib/feature-props.ts`. End-of-file drift guard: `type _MissingReactProps = Exclude<keyof FeatureConfig, keyof FeatureProps>; type _AssertFeaturePropsCoverCore = [_MissingReactProps] extends [never] ? true : ['Missing React props for core features:', _MissingReactProps];`
- INVARIANT: forward-only by design. React props are intentionally richer than core configs — shorthand union types (`'cell' | 'row' | SelectionConfig`), React-node renderers, plus React-only options (`SSRProps.ssr`). Reverse direction (extra React props not in core) is fine and not checked.
- DECIDED (May 2026): if a new feature is added to core's `FeatureConfig` (via plugin augmentation) and not exposed as a React prop, the build fails. Vue/Angular adapters do NOT have an equivalent guard yet — same forward check would work.
- DECIDED (May 2026, deprecated): `SSRProps.ssr` is `@deprecated`. The original RFC introduced it to disable dynamic feature imports during SSR, but the React adapter no longer uses dynamic imports — features are registered via synchronous side-effect imports (SSR-safe by construction). Setting `ssr={true}` today only skips React-side plugin instantiation; `<tbw-grid>` is a custom element that requires a custom-elements polyfill to render server-side regardless of the flag, and on client hydration the grid mounts and renders normally. To be removed in a future major; if real SSR support is needed, design a proper cross-adapter story (hydration handoff, polyfill recommendation, fallback HTML).

## angular-react-vue-parity (May 2026 audit)

- DECIDED (May 2026, parity sweep, Pass 1 — events): Vue and Angular adapters were missing 10 events that React already exposed: `cellCancel`, `editOpen`, `beforeEditClose`, `editClose`, `dirtyChange`, `dataChange`, `columnResizeReset`, `groupExpand`, `groupCollapse`, `contextMenuOpen`. Added to both. Vue's previous `'undo-redo'` listener was a latent bug — the `UndoRedoPlugin` emits separate `'undo'` and `'redo'` events; replaced with two distinct `defineEmits` entries.
- DECIDED (May 2026, parity sweep, Pass 2 — column shorthand): mirrored `libs/grid-vue/src/lib/column-shorthand.ts` to `libs/grid-angular/src/lib/column-shorthand.ts` (`ColumnShorthand`, `parseColumnShorthand`, `normalizeColumns`, `applyColumnDefaults`, `hasColumnShorthands`). Angular `Grid` directive's `columns` input widened to `ColumnShorthand<any>[]` and a new `columnDefaults` input added; both flow through `normalizeColumns` → `applyColumnDefaults` → `adapter.processColumn` in the columns sync effect.
- DECIDED (May 2026, parity sweep, Pass 3 — cardRowHeight): added `cardRowHeight = input<number | 'auto'>()` to `GridResponsiveCard` directive. Mirrored to the `card-row-height` attribute on the underlying `<tbw-grid-responsive-card>` element via an effect (matches Vue's `TbwGridResponsiveCard.vue` pattern).
- DECIDED (May 2026, parity sweep, Pass 4 — plugin access): added `getPlugin<T>(pluginClass)` and `getPluginByName(name)` to `injectGrid()`'s `InjectGridReturn`. Both passthrough to the underlying grid element with safe-nav for the not-yet-ready case (matches React's `useGrid` and Vue's `useGrid`).
- DECIDED (May 2026, parity sweep, Pass 5 — combined provider): added `provideGrid({ typeDefaults, icons })` in `libs/grid-angular/src/lib/grid-provider.ts`. Calls `provideGridTypeDefaults` and/or `provideGridIcons` based on which fields are supplied; returns combined `EnvironmentProviders`. This is the Angular-idiomatic equivalent of Vue's `<GridProvider>` component (Vue uses a renderless wrapper because Vue's provide/inject is component-scoped, Angular uses `EnvironmentProviders` because it's environment-scoped). FeatureProps/AllFeatureProps types are deliberately NOT mirrored to Angular — Angular configures features via individual signal inputs on the `Grid` directive, not a unified prop interface, so the type would have no consumer.
- DECIDED (May 2026, parity sweep, Pass 6 — Angular drift guard): added a forward-only drift guard inside `Grid.directive.ts` that fails to compile if any `keyof DataGridEventMap` is missing from `eventOutputMap`. Pattern: `private declare _intentionallyOmittedEvents: never; private declare _assertEventOutputMapCoversCore: [Exclude<keyof DataGridEventMap<unknown>, (typeof Grid.prototype.eventOutputMap)[keyof typeof Grid.prototype.eventOutputMap] | Grid['_intentionallyOmittedEvents']>] extends [never] ? true : ['Missing Angular outputs for core grid events:', ...]`. The `_intentionallyOmittedEvents: never` field is the documented escape hatch — widen it to a union of event-name string literals if you ever need to consciously skip wiring an output.
- INVARIANT: when a core event is added (registered in `DataGridEventMap` via plugin augmentation), all three adapters must wire it: React `EVENT_PROP_MAP` + `EventProps`, Vue `EVENT_MAP` + `defineEmits`, Angular `eventOutputMap` + `output<T>()` declaration. The two drift guards (React forward via `Record<keyof EventProps, ...>`, Angular forward via `_assertEventOutputMapCoversCore`) catch the React/Angular sides at build time. Vue today has no equivalent guard — adding one would require keeping a `keyof DataGridEventMap`-derived sentinel alongside `defineEmits` (worth a follow-up).
- INVARIANT: missing core public exports surface as Angular build failures, not Vue/React build failures. `libs/grid/src/public.ts` is the only re-export Angular's `@toolbox-web/grid/all` deep-import path can pick up — adding a new event detail type to a plugin's `types.ts` requires also adding it to `public.ts`'s alphabetically-sorted detail-type export list (e.g. `ColumnResizeResetDetail` was registered in the event map but missing from `public.ts`, blocking Angular build until exported).
- INVARIANT (Angular ColumnConfig flavour): the Angular `ColumnConfig` type (from `angular-column-config.ts`) extends the core type by allowing `Type<CellRenderer>` / `Type<CellEditor>` / `Type<FilterPanel>` for renderer-shaped fields. Helpers typed against the core `ColumnConfig` (like `applyColumnDefaults`) cannot accept the Angular flavour at the type level, even though they work at runtime — `processColumn` resolves the component classes downstream. Cast to `any` at the boundary (the columns-sync effect) and let `processColumn` handle the Angular-specific normalization.

## angular-feature-extension-registries (May 2026)

- DECIDED (May 2026): feature-specific bridging code that previously lived in `Grid.directive.ts` (`configureMasterDetail`, `configureResponsiveCard`, hardcoded `processGroupingColumnsConfig` / `processGroupingRowsConfig` / `processPinnedRowsConfig` branches inside `createFeaturePlugins`) was moved out into the corresponding feature secondary entries via two new module-scoped registries in `libs/grid-angular/src/lib/internal/feature-extensions.ts`:
  - `registerTemplateBridge(bridge)` / `runTemplateBridges({ grid, adapter })` — invoked once in `ngAfterContentInit` (inside the existing `setTimeout`). Used by `master-detail` and `responsive` feature secondary entries to wire `<tbw-grid-detail>` / `<tbw-grid-responsive-card>` templates into the running plugin instance (via `MasterDetailPlugin#refreshDetailRenderer()` / `ResponsivePlugin#setCardRenderer()`).
  - `registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor(name, fn)` / `getFeatureConfigPreprocessor(name)` — looked up in `addPlugin()` inside `createFeaturePlugins` to transform a feature config before it's instantiated. Used by `grouping-columns`, `grouping-rows`, `pinned-rows` to bridge embedded Angular component classes via `adapter.processGroupingColumnsConfig` / etc.
    Both registries are append-only and populated by side-effect imports from feature secondary entries; the directive itself has zero knowledge of which features have registered bridges.
- DECIDED (May 2026): removed the dynamic `await import('@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/master-detail')` plugin auto-add path in `configureMasterDetail`. Users must now opt in by either (a) using the `[masterDetail]` feature input on `<tbw-grid>`, or (b) manually instantiating and including `MasterDetailPlugin` in `gridConfig.plugins`. The feature secondary entry's template bridge logs a warning if it finds a `<tbw-grid-detail>` slot element but no matching plugin. Rationale: the auto-add path made the core directive depend on a specific plugin's runtime, defeating tree-shaking, and it also meant the plugin would silently appear without a clear opt-in. Net code removed: ~96 lines from `grid.directive.ts`.
- INVARIANT: feature secondary entries import the registry functions from `@toolbox-web/grid-angular` (the primary entry's package name). Secondary→primary is a standard ng-packagr pattern; primary MUST NOT import from any secondary (would create a build cycle).
- INVARIANT: feature template bridges look up plugins by `name === '<feature-name>'` on `gridConfig.plugins[]` — they MUST NOT import the plugin class itself, because that would defeat tree-shaking when the user only uses the manual-plugin path. Local minimal `PluginLike` interfaces describe the bridge API.
- INVARIANT: `runTemplateBridges` runs all registered bridges concurrently and catches errors per-bridge, so a single faulty bridge cannot break the rest. `getFeatureConfigPreprocessor` returns `undefined` if no preprocessor is registered (the addPlugin path then passes the raw config through unchanged).
- TENSION: this pattern is Angular-specific. React and Vue don't need an equivalent because their template/slot bridging is per-frame primitive — React uses Portals into cell containers, Vue uses teleport via slot registries — both of which are component-scoped and don't require the `ngAfterContentInit` orchestration pass. Angular needs registries because `<ng-template>` registration is content-projection-based AND template parsing has to wait until after content init, not just after view init.

## adapter-internal-helpers (May 2026)

- DECIDED (May 2026, simplification sweep): each adapter has its own small private helper for the boilerplate that previously appeared inline at every renderer/editor wrapping site:
  - `createPortalContainer(className)` in `libs/grid-react/src/lib/react-column-config.ts`
  - `createTeleportContainer(className)` in `libs/grid-vue/src/lib/vue-grid-adapter.ts`
  - `makeFlushFocusedInput(container)` in both adapters
    These are deliberately NOT extracted to a shared package — they're 3-5 lines each, and duplicating them keeps each adapter's bundle independently tree-shakeable. Same rationale as `column-shorthand.ts`.
- DECIDED (May 2026): React's `clearFieldRegistries` export was removed (unused — no source, test, or external import referenced it). Vue's equivalent is kept because Vue tests use it. Lesson: when copying a registry pattern across adapters, audit which exports each adapter actually needs rather than mirroring blindly.
- DECIDED (May 2026): React's `featureKeys` array was hoisted from the `<DataGrid>` render function to module scope as `FEATURE_KEYS` (matching its existing comment that already claimed it was hoisted). Avoids per-render allocation of a 24-element string array; module-scope allocation happens once at import time.
- INVARIANT: in Vue's `vue-grid-adapter.ts`, `createEditor` (the slot-path editor wrapper) MUST resolve `gridEl` eagerly at adapter-creation time via `element.closest('tbw-grid')`, not via `attachBeforeEditCloseFlush`'s `queueMicrotask` path. Reason: tests (and conceivably user code) dispatch `before-edit-close` synchronously during the same task as the editor's first render; the microtask path would install the listener too late and miss the event. The `createConfig*Editor` family (config-based editors) MUST use the microtask path because their containers are constructed before being attached to the DOM. This split is intentional, not a bug. Both paths share the same `makeFlushFocusedInput` helper for the actual blur logic.

## adapter-feature-bridge-registries (May 2026)

- DECIDED (May 2026, react-vue-feature-bridges): React's and Vue's adapters expose two append-only bridge registries — `registerDetailRendererBridge` and `registerResponsiveCardRendererBridge` — populated by the corresponding feature secondary entry (`features/master-detail`, `features/responsive`) on side-effect import. The adapter's `parseDetailElement`, `parseResponsiveCardElement`, `createDetailRenderer`, `createResponsiveCardRenderer` methods stay on the adapter (no breaking change to the `FrameworkAdapter` contract) but their bodies are now thin delegates that call the registered bridge with a `FeatureBridgeContext` (`trackPortal` for React / `trackTeleportKey` for Vue). Mirrors Angular's Pass A/B `registerTemplateBridge` / `registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor` registries — the adapter is the contract surface; features own the bridging implementation.
- INVARIANT: bridge installer functions live in `libs/grid-{react,vue}/src/features/<name>.ts`. The adapter file (`react-grid-adapter.ts` / `vue-grid-adapter.ts`) MUST NOT import from feature secondary entries — features import from the adapter, never the reverse, to avoid build cycles.
- INVARIANT: when no feature is imported, the adapter's `parseDetailElement` / `parseResponsiveCardElement` returns `undefined`. This is non-breaking because in any working configuration the user already imports the feature (otherwise the corresponding plugin is unregistered and `createPluginsFromFeatures` errors with TBW031). The bridge install and the plugin registration share the same import gate.
- INVARIANT: tests that exercise `parseDetailElement` / `parseResponsiveCardElement` directly MUST add side-effect imports of `'../features/master-detail'` / `'../features/responsive'` at the top of the spec file, mirroring how a real consumer would import the feature. Otherwise the bridge is `null` and the methods return `undefined`. Vue's `vue-grid-adapter.spec.ts` does this; React's adapter spec doesn't currently test these methods directly.
- DECIDED: filter-panel renderer wrapping (`createFilterPanelRenderer`, used internally by `getTypeDefault`) was NOT moved to a bridge registry. The runtime body is generic node-to-container wrapping with no feature-specific dependencies — its only filtering-feature import (`FilterPanelParams`) is type-only and adds zero bundle. Moving it would add complexity without architectural or bundle benefit.
- DECIDED: the editor blur bridge (`makeFlushFocusedInput` + `before-edit-close` listener installed in `createEditor`) stays on the adapter. It's a per-editor primitive that ALL editors (slot-based, config-based, type-default) rely on — not editing-feature-specific. Moving it would break editors regardless of how they were constructed.
- TENSION: bundle savings from this refactor are minimal (~hundreds of bytes) because `data-grid.tsx` still imports `getResponsiveCardRenderer` and the detail-panel types for its own DOM-detection helpers (`refreshMasterDetailRenderer`, `refreshResponsiveCardRenderer`, `detectChildComponentFeatures`). The win is architectural separation, not bytes — the adapter no longer carries master-detail / responsive knowledge.

## adapter-feature-purity (May 2026)

- DECIDED (May 2026, react-vue-feature-purity): the React and Vue adapters' core source files (`react-grid-adapter.ts`, `vue-grid-adapter.ts`, `react-column-config.ts`) MUST NOT contain runtime references to feature-specific behaviour. Type-only imports from feature subpaths are fine (zero bundle impact). Runtime feature wiring lives exclusively in the corresponding `features/<name>.ts` secondary entry, which augments the adapter via registered bridges/hooks. This mirrors the webcomponent grid's discipline (core has no plugin knowledge; plugins augment via `registerPlugin()`).
- Editor lifecycle hook system (`libs/grid-{react,vue}/src/lib/editor-mount-hooks.ts`):
  - Append-only `registerEditorMountHook(hook)` registry.
  - Both slot-path (`createEditor`) and config-path (`wrapReactEditor` / `attachBeforeEditCloseFlush`) call `notifyEditorMounted(container, gridEl)` which runs all registered hooks and returns a combined teardown.
  - `features/editing` is the sole installer; it registers a hook that wires `before-edit-close` → native `.blur()` on the focused input. Without that import, no listener is attached — but EditingPlugin requires the same import to register at all (TBW031), so the precondition is unified.
  - Vue's editor-mount-hooks module also owns `makeFlushFocusedInput` (was a private function in `vue-grid-adapter.ts`); the adapter still re-exports it `@deprecated` for any internal callers.
- Filter-panel type-default bridge:
  - `registerFilterPanelTypeDefaultBridge(bridge)` registry on each adapter; bridge signature `(renderFn, gridEl, FeatureBridgeContext) => NonNullable<TypeDefault['filterPanelRenderer']>`.
  - Adapter's `getTypeDefault` (and Vue's `processConfig` for grid-config-level type-defaults) calls the bridge if registered; otherwise drops the user's `filterPanelRenderer` silently.
  - `features/filtering` installs the bridge; the existing top-level `registerFeature('filtering', ...)` filter-panel wrapping for `config.filterPanelRenderer` continues to live in the same file.
- INVARIANT: features import from the adapter's primary entry (registry hooks + types). The adapter primary entry MUST NOT import from feature secondary entries (would create build cycles and re-introduce feature coupling).
- INVARIANT: the only runtime imports in `react-grid-adapter.ts` / `vue-grid-adapter.ts` are local-package modules (`portal-bridge`/`teleport-bridge`, `editor-mount-hooks`, type registries, column-config, tool-panel registry) and Vue's runtime (`createVNode`). All feature-package imports are type-only. Re-audit before merging any change that adds an `import { ... } from '@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/...'` or `from '@toolbox-web/grid/features/...'` to the adapter.
- INVARIANT: tests that exercise behaviour installed by feature secondary entries (`before-edit-close` flush, type-default `filterPanelRenderer`, `parseDetailElement`, `parseResponsiveCardElement`) MUST add side-effect imports of the corresponding `'../features/<name>'` at the top of the spec file. Real consumers achieve the same by importing the feature subpath in their app. Both `react-grid-adapter.spec.ts` / `react-column-config.spec.ts` and `vue-grid-adapter.spec.ts` do this.
- DECIDED: `createToolPanelRenderer` stays on the adapter as a contract method. Tool panels are a CORE primitive (the grid core itself parses `<tbw-grid-tool-panel>` in `shell.ts` and calls `adapter.createToolPanelRenderer` from `grid.ts`); features like column-toggler USE tool panels but tool-panel rendering is not gated on any specific feature. Analogous to `createRenderer` for column cells.
- DECIDED: `createEditor` and `createRenderer` stay on the adapter as required `FrameworkAdapter` contract methods. Their bodies are per-editor / per-cell primitives (build container, render React/Vue, return DOM), not editing-feature-specific. Editing-feature-specific behaviour (the `before-edit-close` listener) was extracted into the editor-mount-hook system above.
- TENSION: bundle savings from this refactor are minor (~hundreds of bytes per adapter index.js). The win is architectural purity: adapter source files no longer carry feature knowledge, so adding a new feature never requires touching the adapter, and dropping a feature has zero surface area in the adapter. This is the same maintenance posture the webcomponent grid achieved via plugins.

## adapter-feature-purity-angular-tier1 (May 2026)

- DECIDED: applied the editor-mount-hook pattern to the Angular adapter to mirror React/Vue. New module `libs/grid-angular/src/lib/editor-mount-hooks.ts` exports `registerEditorMountHook`, `notifyEditorMounted`, and `makeFlushFocusedInput`. The blur listener that flushes pending input on `before-edit-close` is now installed by `@toolbox-web/grid-angular/features/editing` instead of by the adapter itself. Re-exported from the package primary entry so feature subpaths can use them; field renamed `editorBeforeCloseUnsubs` → `editorMountTeardowns` and method `attachBeforeEditCloseFlush` → `runEditorMountHooks`. Both call sites in `createEditor` and `createComponentEditor` updated.
- INVARIANT: Angular feature secondary entries that need helpers from the adapter MUST go through `@toolbox-web/grid-angular` (the package barrel), NOT relative paths like `../../../src/lib/...`. Each Angular feature is a separate ng-packagr secondary entry with its own bundle; relative imports would inline a duplicate module with separate registry state and the bridge would never fire. React/Vue features can use relative `../lib/...` because they ship as a single package.
- INVARIANT: Spec files that exercise blur-bridge behaviour must NOT import the editing feature (`@toolbox-web/grid-angular/features/editing`) as a side-effect — that transitively imports `@toolbox-web/grid/features/editing` which defines the `tbw-grid` custom element and triggers `ResizeObserver` wiring on `document.createElement('tbw-grid')` calls in jsdom. Instead, register the hook inline in `beforeAll` using direct imports from `./editor-mount-hooks` (NOT from `@toolbox-web/grid-angular`, which goes through the barrel and pulls in directives that also drag in the grid). See `angular-grid-adapter.spec.ts:before-edit-close blur bridge`.
- TENSION: Angular adapter still has feature-coupled methods that did NOT move in this Tier 1 pass: `createDetailRenderer` + `parseDetailElement`, `createResponsiveCardRenderer` + `parseResponsiveCardElement`, `createComponentFilterPanelRenderer` (called from `processTypeDefaults` and `getTypeDefault`), and the three feature-config preprocessors `processGroupingColumnsConfig` / `processGroupingRowsConfig` / `processPinnedRowsConfig`. Moving these to features requires exposing several `@internal` primitives — `mountComponentRenderer`, `createComponentGroupHeaderRenderer`, `createComponentGroupRowRenderer`, `createComponentPinnedRowsPanelRenderer` — from the package barrel so feature secondary entries can compose them. That is a larger API-surface change and is its own commit.
- DECIDED: did NOT rename Vue's `editorBeforeCloseUnsubs` field (or update its now-stale comments) in the same commit. The field is private and its body is already generic (`notifyEditorMounted`); this is cosmetic and can be cleaned up alongside other adapter renames in a separate cosmetic pass.

## adapter-feature-purity-angular-tier2 (May 2026)

- DECIDED: extracted three new bridges in `libs/grid-angular/src/lib/internal/feature-bridges.ts` (`detailRendererBridge`, `responsiveCardRendererBridge`, `filterPanelTypeDefaultBridge`) and re-exported their setters (`registerDetailRendererBridge`, `registerResponsiveCardRendererBridge`, `registerFilterPanelTypeDefaultBridge`) through `angular-grid-adapter.ts` and the package barrel. Adapter methods `createDetailRenderer`, `parseDetailElement`, `createResponsiveCardRenderer`, `parseResponsiveCardElement` and the `processTypeDefaults`/`getTypeDefault` `filterPanelRenderer` branches are now thin delegates that pass through if the corresponding feature is not imported. The bridge state module is intentionally framework-free (no `@angular/core` import), so the type-only `GridAdapter` import doesn't drag Angular runtime into the storage layer.
- DECIDED: reused the existing `registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor` (in `internal/feature-extensions.ts`) for grouping/pinned-rows. Adapter's `processGroupingColumnsConfig`, `processGroupingRowsConfig`, `processPinnedRowsConfig` are now one-liner delegates: `return this.applyFeatureConfigPreprocessor('<name>', config);`. The full bridging bodies (component-class → renderer function) live in `features/grouping-columns`, `features/grouping-rows`, `features/pinned-rows`. No new bridge type was introduced.
- DECIDED: promoted `mountComponentRenderer` from private to public `@internal` and added a new public `@internal` method `createTrackedEmbeddedView<TCtx>(template, context)` that wraps `viewContainerRef.createEmbeddedView` + view-ref tracking + `detectChanges`. Feature subpaths use these to mount Angular components/templates as cell/panel renderers without reaching into adapter privates. `setComponentInputs` stayed private. Adapter no longer imports anything from `@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/*` — only types from `@toolbox-web/grid` core.
- DECIDED: also re-exported `getDetailTemplate` and `getResponsiveCardTemplate` from the package barrel (they were already exported from their respective directive modules but not from the index). Feature subpaths need them to resolve user-provided templates inside the bridge installer.
- INVARIANT: Angular feature secondary entries MUST NOT relative-import outside their own `rootDir` (e.g. `../../../src/lib/...`) for VALUE imports. ng-packagr fails the build with `TS6059: File ... is not under 'rootDir' '...features/<name>/src'` — the Angular template-typecheck step generates `.ngtypecheck.ts` files for every reachable directive, and those land outside the secondary entry's rootDir. Always import via `@toolbox-web/grid-angular` (the package barrel). Type-only imports may use relative paths because they are erased before the program graph is materialised, but value imports cannot. (Tier 1 already documented the duplicate-registry hazard; this entry adds the rootDir hazard.)
- INVARIANT: Spec files that import a feature subpath via the package barrel (e.g. `features/filtering/src/index.spec.ts`) MUST mock the full set of Angular peer modules the barrel pulls in: `@angular/core` decorator factories (`Component`, `Directive`, `Injectable`, `Pipe`, `NgModule`, `Input`, `Output`, …) plus signal helpers (`input`, `output`, `contentChild`, `viewChild`, `effect`, `computed`), runtime types (`TemplateRef`, `ViewContainerRef`, `EnvironmentInjector`, `ApplicationRef`, `EmbeddedViewRef`, `ComponentRef`, `EventEmitter`, `InjectionToken`), `createComponent`, `makeEnvironmentProviders`, AND the transitively-loaded peers `@angular/forms` (FormGroup/FormArray/AbstractControl/FormControl/Validators), `@angular/common` (CommonModule/NgIf/NgForOf/AsyncPipe), and `@angular/core/rxjs-interop` (`takeUntilDestroyed`, `toSignal`, `toObservable`). Decorator factories must return a no-op decorator: `(...args) => (target) => target`. See `features/filtering/src/index.spec.ts`.
- INVARIANT: `angular-grid-adapter.spec.ts` registers all bridges + feature-config preprocessors inline in `beforeAll` (not via side-effect `import '@toolbox-web/grid-angular/features/...'`, which would define `tbw-grid` and trip ResizeObserver in jsdom). The bridge setters are imported directly from `./internal/feature-bridges` and `./internal/feature-extensions`, NOT through the package barrel.
- TENSION: Tier 2 frees the adapter from feature-specific code but at the cost of API surface (`mountComponentRenderer`, `createTrackedEmbeddedView` are now `@internal` public methods). They are documented as `@internal` but TypeScript can't enforce that — third-party consumers could call them. Acceptable trade-off given they are namespaced under `@internal` and the alternative is keeping all feature wiring inside the adapter.
- TENSION: Bundle savings from Tier 2 are marginal because grouping/pinned-rows preprocessors were already small. The motivation is architectural purity (adapter has zero feature-name knowledge except `FeatureName` type for the `applyFeatureConfigPreprocessor` helper), not bytes.

## angular-directive-real-class-spec-pattern (May 2026)

- DECIDED: directives that depend on Angular DI primitives (`inject`, `effect`, `input`, `input.required`, `output`, `afterNextRender`) are unit-tested by mocking those primitives via `vi.mock('@angular/core', …)` and instantiating the class with `new`. No TestBed. The reference implementations are `directives/grid-form-array.directive.spec.ts` and `directives/grid-lazy-form.directive.spec.ts` (the previous mock-only specs that never imported the real classes were deleted in May 2026). Mock `inject` with a per-test `mockInjectResolver` swap; mock `input` / `input.required` with a `(initial?) => () => value` factory that exposes `__setValue`; mock `output` with `() => ({ emit, emissions: [] })` so tests can assert emissions; mock `afterNextRender` with `(cb) => { pending = cb; }` and trigger `pending()` manually. Mock `effect` with a no-op when the directive uses effects only for side-effect plumbing.
- INVARIANT: When mocking `@angular/core` for a directive spec, ALWAYS spread `await vi.importActual(...)` first and override only the primitives the directive uses. Without the actual exports, `@angular/forms` (which spec helpers may import) breaks because `EventEmitter`, `InjectionToken`, etc. are missing.
- INVARIANT: Real-class directive specs MUST import `'@angular/compiler'` at the top before any other Angular import. Without it, vitest fails with "JIT compilation failed" the first time a directive class is instantiated, even though no template compilation actually happens in the spec.
- INVARIANT: `getOrCreate*` lazy patterns (e.g. `GridLazyForm.#getOrCreateFormGroup`) are exercised by first calling a public method that triggers materialisation (`ctx.getRowFormGroup(0)`) and then asserting cache state via `directive.getAllFormGroups().size` or by re-calling and verifying the factory was only invoked once.
- DECIDED: `coverage.exclude` is NOT used to prop up the 70% threshold. When the Tier 2 refactor expanded v8's measurement scope and exposed the gap, the response was to write real specs (`directives/grid-lazy-form.directive.spec.ts`, +65 tests, lifted `grid-lazy-form.directive.ts` from 2.77% → 98.33%), not to hide the files. Still-uncovered directive files (`grid.directive.ts` 1.85%, `grid-tool-panel.directive.ts` 32%, `grid-column-{view,editor}.directive.ts` 53%, `structural-directives.ts` 46%, `grid-detail-view.directive.ts` 71%, `grid-responsive-card.directive.ts` 41%) are honest TODOs — when their unit coverage is needed, the same real-class pattern applies.
- TENSION: The 70% threshold currently passes (70.68/70.64/75.81/71.29 as of May 2026) but with very little headroom. Any further code added to `grid.directive.ts` without matching specs will likely re-fail CI. Long-term plan: write a real-class spec for `grid.directive.ts` itself (it's 1581 lines and will need ~600-800 lines of spec to land near the per-file 70% bar) following the same pattern.

## ng-packagr-secondary-entry-rules (May 2026)

- INVARIANT: ng-packagr 21 forbids the **primary** entry from depending on a **secondary** entry. The dependency direction is one-way: secondary → primary. This blocks the otherwise-natural pattern of "physically move the directive into the feature folder, then re-export it from the main barrel".
- INVARIANT: Re-exporting a directive from a sibling source file via a relative path (`export { GridDetailView } from '../features/master-detail/src/...'`) does compile, but ng-packagr emits the file into BOTH bundles. You then ship two distinct directive classes with the same selector and two distinct module-level registries (e.g. `detailTemplateRegistry`), so the cross-bundle bridge stops working at runtime. NEVER use this pattern.
- DECIDED: feature-specific directives that we WISH lived in their feature folder but cannot move yet (e.g. `GridDetailView` belongs to master-detail) are handled with a soft-deprecation pattern: source file stays in `src/lib/directives/`, main `src/index.ts` re-exports it with `@deprecated` JSDoc pointing at the feature entry, the feature entry re-exports the same symbols from `@toolbox-web/grid-angular` so the canonical import path already works. Physical move is queued for the next major (v2.0.0) where the deprecated re-export is dropped.
- DECIDED: `getDetailConfig` (read `showExpandColumn` / `animation` attributes from the DOM) was deleted in May 2026 — it was exported from `grid-detail-view.directive.ts` but never used by any production code. The plugin reads the `GridDetailView` directive's `input()` signals directly via `parseLightDomDetail`. Removing it dropped 16 lines from the directive file and 11 obsolete spec assertions.

## tsconfig-path-mappings-and-typedoc (May 2026)

- INVARIANT: `tsconfig.base.json` maps `@toolbox-web/grid-angular` → `dist/libs/grid-angular/index.d.ts` and `@toolbox-web/grid-angular/features/*` → `dist/libs/grid-angular/features/*.d.ts`. Both paths are STALE — ng-packagr 21 emits to `dist/libs/grid-angular/types/toolbox-web-grid-angular.d.ts` and `dist/libs/grid-angular/types/toolbox-web-grid-angular-features-<name>.d.ts`. The mapping has been broken since the ng-packagr migration (`5a6f3fb4`); most consumers never noticed because they read TS sources directly via project references or the `paths` value is overridden by `package.json#exports` at runtime. Do not "fix" the global mapping without auditing every typecheck/typedoc/build target — multiple targets currently work _because_ the mapping resolves to nothing.
- DECIDED: `libs/grid-angular/tsconfig.typedoc.json` overrides `paths` to point at SOURCE (`./src/index.ts`, `./features/*/src/index.ts`) so typedoc never depends on a built dist. Without this override, typedoc fails with 30+ TS2307 cascades because feature `index.ts` files import from `@toolbox-web/grid-angular`. Mirror this pattern for any future "read source across primary/secondary boundary" tooling.
- INVARIANT: `libs/grid-react/src/lib/column-shorthand.ts` MUST import `ColumnConfig` from `./react-column-config`, NOT from `@toolbox-web/grid`. The React-shaped `ColumnConfig` widens `renderer` etc. to `(ctx) => ReactNode`. If `column-shorthand` uses the core type, `applyColumnDefaults(normalizeColumns(columns), columnDefaults)` in `data-grid.tsx` triggers TS2345 (ReactNode → ColumnViewRenderer). Mirror in `grid-vue` (Vue-shaped) and `grid-angular` (TemplateRef-shaped) when those adapters add column-defaults support.
